title: 11 Herbal Creams For Jaw Lock
intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaw lock are becoming increasingly popular, and for good reason. These creams often contain soothing herbs like Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease jaw tension. When we're stressed or anxious, our muscles can lock up, causing jaw pain and discomfort. Herbal teas made with these same herbs can also provide relief, by allowing us to relax and unwind. For example, drinking a tea made with Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, can help calm our minds and ease tension in the jaw. Another herb, Zingiber officinale, or ginger, can also help to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When we're feeling stressed, our bodies are also more likely to hold onto tension, which can lead to digestive issues, headaches, and other problems. By using herbal teas or creams to relieve jaw lock, we can break this cycle of tension and start to feel more relaxed and centered. This can have a ripple effect, improving our mood, energy levels, and even our relationships with others. By incorporating these herbal remedies into our daily routine, we can start to feel more balanced and in control, and say goodbye to jaw lock and other related issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains active constituents like thymol, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the jaw and relieve tension. Thymol, in particular, has antioxidant properties that can help protect the jaw muscles from damage. The anti-inflammatory properties of salvia officinalis creams can also help relax the jaw muscles, which can become tense and lock up due to stress or anxiety. The ursolic acid in the creams can also help reduce pain and inflammation in the jaw, making it a potential treatment for jaw lock.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil over low heat, then add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat until melted.
Combine the strained Salvia officinalis mixture with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well. Pour into a container and let cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthone and menthol, which are the main bioactive constituents that help with jaw lock. Menthone has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area. Menthol, on the other hand, acts as a natural analgesic, numbing the pain and relaxing the jaw muscles. The menthol also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce tension and spasms in the jaw. By applying Mentha x piperita creams topically, menthone and menthol can work together to provide quick relief from jaw lock symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval.
Once the mixture is melted, remove it from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add the peppermint essential oil and vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden completely before using it to relieve jaw lock.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw, making it a good remedy for jaw lock. The warming and analgesic effects of ginger also help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate jaw tension. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of ginger in Zingiber officinale creams may help prevent infections that can cause jaw pain. By reducing inflammation and easing pain, Zingiber officinale creams can help provide relief from jaw lock and promote overall comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger oil, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in ginger oil and peppermint essential oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use as a cream to relieve jaw lock.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains eucalyptol, a compound that helps to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the jaw area. Eucalyptol also has a decongestant effect, which can help to clear mucus from the sinuses and reduce pressure on the jaw joint. The camphor in Eucalyptus globulus creams has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the jaw muscles. The menthol in the cream can help to cool and numb the area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort. By applying Eucalyptus globulus creams to the jaw area, these active constituents can work together to help relax the muscles and reduce tension.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Melt 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add 1/2 cup of beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until melted.
Add 10 drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and 1 tablespoon of peppermint oil to the mixture.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool completely before use as a jaw lock cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhetic acid and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw. Glycyrrhetic acid also has a relaxing effect on muscles, which can help alleviate tension and spasms that cause jaw locking. The saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have a soothing effect on the skin and mucous membranes, which can help calm irritation and discomfort in the jaw area. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help reduce frequency and severity of jaw locking episodes by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the jaw muscles.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 10-15 seconds.
In a separate bowl, mix the distilled water and glycerin. Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and stir well.
Slowly add the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture to the water mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and harden. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant, and carnosic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area, making them effective in treating jaw lock. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid also help to reduce tension and inflammation in the temporomandibular joint, which can contribute to jaw lock. Rosmarinus officinalis creams also contain camphor, which has a cooling effect that can help to numb the pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams to the jaw area, you can experience relief from the pain and discomfort associated with jaw lock.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 10 drops of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Add vitamin E oil and Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Use as a cream for jaw lock by applying a small amount to the affected area and massaging gently.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two key active constituents known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw area, making them effective in treating jaw lock. Linalool, in particular, has a relaxing effect on muscle spasms, which can contribute to jaw tension. The anti-inflammatory properties of linalyl acetate help to reduce redness and swelling in the jaw area, promoting a faster recovery. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams, you can help to ease the discomfort and tension associated with jaw lock.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in lavender essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches a temperature of 120°F to 130°F. This is an important step to prevent bacterial growth.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to relieve jaw lock and tension.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains a mixture of biologically active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the jaw muscles. The sesquiterpene lactones, particularly achillin and millefolin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and pain in the jaw area. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Achillea millefolium creams also have antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Achillea millefolium creams may help to alleviate jaw lock and promote a sense of relief and comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. Chop them into small pieces.
Combine 1/4 cup of chopped herbs with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil mixture. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the wax and butter melt.
Pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains active constituents like cineole, limonene, and terpineol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and fight infection in the jaw area. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin also makes it effective in reducing redness and promoting healing. The antimicrobial properties of terpineol, in particular, help to combat the underlying causes of jaw lock, such as bacterial or fungal infections. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can provide relief from jaw lock symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax in the double boiler, then add 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes, then add 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Whisk the mixture until it's smooth, then add 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before using it on your jaw lock.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoids, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which are potent anti-inflammatory compounds. These constituents help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area, making it a good remedy for jaw lock. The anti-inflammatory properties of triterpenoids, such as calendulic acid, help combat inflammation and irritation in the jaw joint and surrounding tissues. Flavonoids, like quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help protect the jaw tissues from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Calendula officinalis creams can help alleviate the symptoms of jaw lock and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers. Measure 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Mix 1 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of witch hazel in a small bowl. Steep the Calendula flowers in the mixture for 10 minutes.
Strain the Calendula mixture and discard the flowers. Combine the strained mixture with beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let cool completely. Once set, your Calendula cream for jaw lock is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains boiactive constituents such as hyperforin and hypericin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area. The flavonoids in the cream, particularly rutin and quercetin, can also help reduce inflammation and promote healing. The antispasmodic properties of Hypericum perforatum can help relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw locking episodes. By reducing inflammation and promoting muscle relaxation, Hypericum perforatum creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with jaw locking.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for jaw lock
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Hypericum perforatum (St. John's Wort) flowers and leaves. Chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped herb with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat it over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the bowl. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, it's ready to use for treating jaw lock.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea, especially those containing chamomile or peppermint, may help relax the jaw muscles. These teas can calm the body and reduce tension, which may prevent jaw lock from forming. Some people find that sipping herbal tea helps them relax and release jaw tension, but it's not a guaranteed solution and results may vary.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for jaw lock every day can be safe, but it depends on the teas you choose. Some herbs like chamomile and peppermint are generally considered gentle and calming. However, others like feverfew and willow bark may be too potent for daily use. Be cautious and monitor your body's response.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with jaw lock, but it depends on the type and how often you drink it. Some people notice relief after a few hours, while others might take a few days. Generally, it can take a couple of days to start feeling better, but it's not the same for everyone.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for jaw lock can be most effective when you have it in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the tea to work throughout your day, helping to ease tension and reduce stiffness in your jaw. Having it at this time can also help you stay focused and relaxed.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for jaw lock, also known as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder. Some herbs like ginger, feverfew, and saw palmetto may slow blood clotting or affect blood pressure, which can be a problem if you're taking medications that also affect blood clotting or pressure.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs like pennyroyal, blue cohosh, and red clover can affect the baby's growth or cause contractions. If you have a jaw lock, try gentle stretches or a warm compress. You can also try warm water or ginger tea, which are generally considered safe during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for jaw lock can be a helpful remedy for breastfeeding mothers. However, some herbs can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. If you choose to try herbal teas, look for ones that are specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended dosage.
